title: Interests
text: When not in school, you can find me running along the bluffs by Devereux Beach or sailing with my team in the Santa Barbara Harbor! I love the outdoors and have a great appreciation for nature and the world around me.

title: Interests
text:
Running
I love to run and post my runs on Strava. I started running freshman year, when I found the trails in the Goleta Monarch Preserve. From there, I challenged myself to run in the mornings and sophmore year, I ran the Santa Barbara Half Marathon. From then, I have been consistently running as a way to start my day right! I also really enjoy the friends I have been able to make through running.



Running with my friend Mia in the Monarch Butterfly Preserve

title: Interests
text:
Sailing
I sail for the UCSB Sailing Team. I started sailing when I was 8 years old and have been doing it ever since. One of the things I love most about sailing in Santa Barbara is how amazing the wildlife is. I have seen dolphins, sea lions, and even whales while out on the water. The sunsets while sailing are also incredible. I love the sport and the community that comes with it.



Sailing a C-Scow in Chautauqua Lake, NY

      About me
      Hi. I'm Cristina. I am intersted in financial management, sustainable energy, and learning to code. I will be graduating this Spring. I enjoy sailing, running, and vibe-coding in my free time.

title: Projects
text:
Economics 199RA: DataHack
In my DataHack course with Camilo Abbate Granada and Jonah Danziger, I worked with a team to analyze data on Medicare in California. This project involved cleaning and analyzing large datasets, visualizing our findings, and presenting our results to the class. Our task was to provide evidence for the following question: What issue should be prioritized by California’s policy makers and why?

Financial Pressure Across Medi-Cal Healthcare Plans

title: Projects
text:
Economics 194S: Group Studies
In my Econ 194S course with Professor Bree Lang, I conducted research on the differences in education across California. This project involved analyzing data on educational attainment, income levels, and other socioeconomic factors to understand how these variables interact and affect educational outcomes. I used R to analyze the data and create visualizations to support my findings. This course taught me to identify and evaluate different empirical research methods in economics, develop a research proposal, apply R programming skills to analyze and visualize results, and to ceate professional research documents using Quarto.

The Effect of Free Child Care on New Mexico Families

title: cristinajacks0n.github.io
text:
Sunset walk to Devs Beach in Isla Vista


Hi! I am Cristina Jackson, an undergraduate student at UC Santa Barbara studying Economics and Environmental Studies. Over this past quarter, I have been learning how to code with R. I have really enjoyed learning. When not in school, you can find me running along the bluffs by Devereux Beach or sailing with my team in the Santa Barbara Harbor! I love the outdoors and one of my favorite things about Santa Barbara is how amazing the geography is, from getting to spend a day on the beach or drive up to the mountains for a sunset. During the summers, I coach sailing at the Chautauqua sailing center, a role I have held for the past few summers and I also currently work in administration at a doctors office in Santa Barbara.

title: About Me
text:
I love to travel, explore new places, and take cool pictures of nature. This year, I went to Laguna Beach with my sister and mom and saw the most amazing sunset. I also went to Joshua Tree for the first time, and thought the rock formations were very unique. Here are some photos from my adventures.



Sunset in Laguna Beach





Rocks in Joshua Tree National Park

title: About Me
text:
Pets
I have two dogs, Ollie and Buster. They are the cutest. Ollie, I like to take on long runs on the beach. Buster loves to go on a walk to Starbucks with me and get a pup cup.



Ollie





Buster





Ollie and his friend Bodie and my friend Mia!

title: Cristina Jackson
text:
Sunset walk to Devs Beach in Isla Vista


Hi! I am Cristina Jackson, an undergraduate student at UC Santa Barbara studying Economics and Environmental Studies. This past quarter, I have been learning how to code with R Studio, which I have really enjoyed because of the creativity and problem solving it allows for. In both my economics and environmental studies courses this quarter, I have used R Studio to analyze data and present my findings with teachers, classmates, and even judges. When not in school, you can find me running along the bluffs by Devereux Beach or sailing with my team in the Santa Barbara Harbor! I love being outdoors and one of my favorite things about Santa Barbara is how there are so many activities to do outside, from getting to spend a day on the beach or drive up to the mountains for a sunset. During the summers, I coach sailing at the Chautauqua sailing center, a role I have held for the past few summers. I also currently work in administration with Doss MD/Artistic Eyes, an oculoplastic surgeon’s office in downtown Santa Barbara.

title: PG&EValuationScenarioAnalysis
text:
Econ 134A · Financial Management
      PG&EValuationScenarioAnalysis
    
    
      PCGTicker
    
  
  
    Overview
    PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) is an energy holding company whose subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric Company, is a major utility company that provides electricity in Northern and Central California.
    In this Scenario analysis, the base case values are directly from Part A, the financial model spreadsheet. The following scenarios presented show 2 bear cases, one in which PG&E is liable vs not liable for a wildfire or natural disaster event. The bull case shows the 'optimistic' scenario of expanded revenue base in the case of AI data center expansion and heightened demand.
    Scroll to walk through each scenario. Select from the boxes below to go directly to a specific scenario.
    
      Overview
      Base Case
      Bear A
      Bear B
      Bull
      Comparison
      Discussion
    
  




  

    
    

    
      Company Overview
      PG&E is a regulated utility and operates under the California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC), which caps PG&E's rate of return at 7.8%. Following its 2019 Chapter 11 bankruptcy, the result of being to blame for the 2017 and 2018 catastrophic wildfires, the company has undertaken a $63B multi-year infrastructure campaign.
      The 2017–2018 wildfires proved that PG&E was due for a major overhaul — its infrastructure was outdated and dangerous. Now, with a new CEO and majority new board members, a plan for improvement centered around sustainability, keeping rates for customers low, and improving efficiency may set PG&E on a more stable future.
      The purpose of this scenario analysis is to show potential future scenarios, and the probability of them occurring, that could affect PG&E.
      The 4 scenarios, including the base case, are described below. From our base case, it is evident that the intrinsic share price differs from the currently traded market price, which demonstrates that investors have a different view on top of our current assumptions that might be driving the share price higher. With our scenario analysis, we might be able to describe the discrepancy between the two prices.
    

    
      Current Market Price$18.36NYSE: PCG · March 10, 2026
      Base Case Intrinsic−$20.81DCF · WACC 5.283% · 27% tax
